topic: OpenAI Codex Skills Guide: Reusable Agent Behaviors for Developer Workflows

key_points: Official OpenAI docs on Codex Skills — defines skills as task-specific packages of instructions, resources, and optional scripts
Skills directory structure: SKILL.md (required) + optional scripts/, references/, assets/
Personal skills at ~/.agents/skills, team/repo skills at .agents/skills
Explains saving recurring workflows as skills — PR review, release checklists, CSV-to-report transforms
Rule of thumb: if you re-use the same prompt or correct the same workflow repeatedly, make it a skill
Skills allow progressive disclosure — Codex only loads the full SKILL.md when it decides to use that skill
Case study: OpenAI used skills to accelerate their own SDK maintenance
457 PRs merged in the two SDK repos between Dec 2025–Feb 2026 using skills-based workflows
Demonstrates skills for: verification, release preparation, integration testing, and PR review
Practical list of 9 must-have skills for Codex in 2026
Covers PR review automation, CI fix, changelog generation, code documentation, and test generation
Emphasizes trigger words in skill descriptions for reliable auto-matching by Codex
Official openai/skills GitHub repo — catalog of ready-to-use skills
Curated skills include: cloudflare-deploy, gh-address-comments, gh-fix-ci, develop-web-game, linear, notion-knowledge-capture, netlify-deploy, pdf, imagegen, jupyter-notebook
Skills also support an agents/openai.yaml sidecar for advanced configuration

stats.fact: Codex reached 4M+ weekly active developers by April 21, 2026 — up from 3M just two weeks earlier
In March 2026, Codex served 2M+ weekly users — a 5x increase in three months, growing 70%+ MoM
Enterprise (ChatGPT Business/Enterprise) Codex users grew 6x between January and April 2026
OpenAI's own SDK repos merged 457 PRs using skills-based workflows in just 3 months (Dec 2025–Feb 2026)
Codex Skills launched as an experimental feature in December 2025 and reached wide availability in early 2026
76% of developers are using or planning to use AI code assistants as of 2024, growing through 2026
Blind code reviewers rated Claude Code output cleaner 67% of the time vs. Codex 25% — but Codex leads Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.7% vs. Claude Opus 4.7

angles: Skills as 'agent macros' — define a workflow once in SKILL.md, invoke it indefinitely without re-prompting
Skills vs. AGENTS.md: AGENTS.md is always-on repository instructions; skills are on-demand, task-specific packages that load only when Codex chooses to use them
Progressive context disclosure: Codex sees only skill name/description/path by default, loading the full SKILL.md only when the skill is invoked — keeps context lean
Three storage tiers: personal (~/.agents/skills), repo-level (.agents/skills checked into git), and the official openai/skills catalog
Curated skills catalog covers real developer pain-points: gh-fix-ci, gh-address-comments, cloudflare-deploy, netlify-deploy, notion-knowledge-capture
Skills + automations = full CI/CD integration: skills define HOW, GitHub Actions automations define WHEN
OSS maintenance case study: 457 PRs merged using skills-workflows, demonstrating 10x productivity gains for open-source teams
Team skills checked into .agents/skills serve as living onboarding docs — new devs inherit all institutional workflows immediately

H2: Skills vs. AGENTS.md — When to Use Each
H2: Anatomy of a SKILL.md File (Structure + Example)
H2: Where to Store Skills: Personal, Repo, and Catalog Tiers
H2: 9 Essential Codex Skills Every Developer Team Needs in 2026
H2: The Official openai/skills Catalog: Curated Skills Breakdown
H2: Building Your First Custom Skill: Step-by-Step
H2: Integrating Skills with GitHub Actions and CI/CD
H2: Real-World Results: How OpenAI Used Skills to Merge 457 PRs
H2: Best Practices for Reliable, Team-Shareable Skills
